title: Kind-alignment matrix (Phase 3a)
description: Schema for kind-alignment.json. Realized kinds -> spec desired kinds. See SEMANTICS.md sec 5.

realized_kind.description: A realized node kind. An edge is written as EDGE:<type> (e.g. EDGE:calls) and is consumed as evidence, not as a desired node.

desired_kind.description: The spec desired kind this realized kind aligns to. Required for exact/approximate/edge_evidence; FORBIDDEN for `wrapper` (a wrapper has no desired kind of its own — it coalesces to its wrapped inner node, which carries the identity+kind).

alignment.description: exact = 1:1 kind correspondence; approximate = mapped but lossy (note required by convention); edge_evidence = realized edge used as evidence for a desired-side check, never a node; wrapper = a syntactic wrapper kind that the extractor emits ALONGSIDE the wrapped inner node — it coalesces to that inner node (SEMANTICS sec 5) and is NOT itself a desired entity, so it carries no desired_kind; structural = an implementation-detail kind (e.g. a body-local variable binding) that is genuinely NEVER a desired entity in a declared syntactic position — it is classified refinement-below-frontier (permitted) when its `structural_condition` holds and FALLS BACK to unmapped-realized-kind -> unsupported otherwise, so it carries no desired_kind.

coalesce.description: Only for alignment=`wrapper`. The coalescing rule: the wrapper coalesces to (is subsumed by) an in-scope realized node that shares its provenance.semantic_path and has a MAPPED non-wrapper kind. If no such inner node exists, the wrapper FALLS BACK to the unmapped-realized-kind rule (-> unsupported); a lone wrapper never silently vanishes.

structural_condition.description: Only for alignment=`structural`. The SYNTACTIC POSITION in which the kind is genuinely below-frontier. body_local = the realized node's PARENT boundary has kind `function` or `method` (the binding is inside a callable body). When the condition does NOT hold (module/class-level), the kind FALLS BACK to the unmapped-realized-kind rule (-> unsupported); the HONESTY guard so a structural kind out of position never manufactures a green.
